extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property is VIOLATED. The interaction_drop rule (priority 100, far higher than any other) forces acuity := self_care (the lowest enum value) whenever severe_headache is true, overriding the higher-acuity rules.

Counterexample (input variable held constant: abdominal_pain=true, stiff_neck=false):
- Baseline severe_headache=false: only raise_abdominal_pain (priority 2) matches, so acuity = urgent.
- Perturbed severe_headache=true (witness assignment): interaction_drop (priority 100) wins over raise_abdominal_pain (priority 2), so acuity = self_care.

Setting severe_headache true lowers acuity from urgent to self_care, which is strictly below the prior value. This breaks the property 'setting severe_headache true never lowers acuity.' (Equivalently, with stiff_neck=true the drop is even larger: emergent -> self_care.) The witness_assignment gives the perturbed input (severe_headache=true) at which the lowered acuity self_care is produced relative to its severe_headache=false baseline.
